title: Palindrome by Splitting

content.problemStatement:
JJ has an array AAA initially of length NNN. He can perform the following operation on AAA:

**1)** Pick any index iii (1≤i≤∣A∣)(1 \le i \le |A|)(1≤i≤∣A∣) such that Ai>1A_i \gt 1Ai​>1 \\
**2)** Select any two integers XXX and YYY such that X+Y=AiX + Y = A_iX+Y=Ai​ and X,Y≥1X, Y \ge 1X,Y≥1 \\
**3)** Replace AiA_iAi​ with XXX and YYY

Note that the length of the array increases by 111 after each operation.

For example, if A=[4,6,7,2]A = [4, 6, 7, 2]A=[4,6,7,2], he can select i=1i = 1i=1 and select X=1,Y=3X = 1, Y = 3X=1,Y=3 (since X+Y=A1X + Y = A_1X+Y=A1​). After the operation array becomes: [4‾,6,7,2]→[1‾,3‾,6,7,2][\underline{4}, 6, 7, 2] \rightarrow [\underline{1}, \underline{3}, 6, 7, 2][4​,6,7,2]→[1​,3​,6,7,2].

JJ wants to make AAA palindromic. Find the minimum number of operations to do so.

It is guaranteed that AAA can be converted to a palindromic array by using the above operation.

Note: An array is called a palindrome if it reads the same backwards and forwards, for e.g. [1,3,3,1][1, 3, 3, 1][1,3,3,1] and [6,2,6][6, 2, 6][6,2,6] are palindromic.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of operations to make AAA palindromic.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 200000
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. Second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer – the minimum number of split operations required to transform the array into a palindrome.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: split 4 into 1+3 (1 op) → [3,7,6,1,3]; split 7 into 3+4 (1 op) → [3,1,6,6,1,3]; palindrome. Total 2.\nTest case 2: already a palindrome, 0 operations.\nTest case 3: four splits are needed as shown in the statement.
A single element array is already a palindrome, so no operations are needed.
Both elements are equal, the array is a palindrome without any operation.

solution.approach: We use a two‑pointer greedy method. Keep pointers i at the start and j at the end. If A[i] == A[j] we move both pointers inward – these elements already match. If A[i] < A[j] we must split the larger element A[j] into two parts, one equal to A[i] (which will match) and the remainder (A[j]‑A[i]) that stays at position j. This costs one operation, we increment i and keep the new value at j. Symmetrically, if A[i] > A[j] we split A[i]. Repeating this until i >= j yields the minimal number of operations because each operation reduces the mismatch as much as possible and never creates extra mismatches. The algorithm runs in O(N) per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input array.

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i is the test case index (0‑based) as defined in hiddenTestCases.
    # The function should print the exact input for that test case.
    if i == 0:  # tc_1
        print(1)
        print(1)
        print(1)
    elif i == 1:  # tc_2
        print(1)
        print(2)
        print('1 2')
    elif i == 2:  # tc_3
        print(1)
        print(2)
        print('2 1')
    elif i == 3:  # tc_4
        print(1)
        print(3)
        print('1 1 1')
    elif i == 4:  # tc_5
        print(1)
        print(5)
        print(' '.join(['100000']*5))
    elif i == 5:  # tc_6
        print(1)
        print(5)
        print('100000 99999 99998 99997 99996')
    elif i == 6:  # tc_7
        print(1)
        print(5)
        print('1 2 3 4 5')
    elif i == 7:  # tc_8
        print(1)
        print(15)
        seq = []
        for _ in range(7):
            seq.extend(['100000','1'])
        seq.append('100000')
        print(' '.join(seq))
    elif i == 8:  # tc_9
        print(1)
        print(10)
        print(' '.join(['7']*10))
    elif i == 9:  # tc_10
        print(1)
        print(6)
        print('1 2 1 2 1 2')
    elif i == 10:  # tc_11
        print(1)
        print(7)
        print('1 2 3 4 5 6 7')
    elif i == 11:  # tc_12
        print(1)
        print(7)
        print('7 6 5 4 3 2 1')
    elif i == 12:  # tc_13
        print(1)
        print(3)
        print('99991 99989 99991')
    elif i == 13:  # tc_14
        print(1)
        print(4)
        print('2 3 5 7')
    elif i == 14:  # tc_15
        print(1)
        print(5)
        print('100000 1 100000 1 100000')
    elif i == 15:  # tc_16
        print(1)
        print(6)
        print('1 100000 1 100000 1 100000')
    elif i == 16:  # tc_17
        print(3)
        print(2)
        print('1 3')
        print(3)
        print('2 2 2')
        print(4)
        print('4 3 2 1')
    elif i == 17:  # tc_18
        print(2)
        print(5)
        print('5 4 3 2 1')
        print(5)
        print('1 2 3 4 5')
    elif i == 18:  # tc_19
        print(1)
        print(15)
        print('12 7 9 3 14 6 2 11 5 8 10 4 13 1 16')
    elif i == 19:  # tc_20
        print(1)
        print(1)
        print('100000')
    else:
        raise ValueError('Invalid test case index')
